# Formal Conjectures PR Checklist ‚ÄĒ `deepmind/1167.lean`

Every item must pass before a PR is approved. Reviewers: reject if any box is unchecked
without an explicit justification.

---

## 1. Copyright Header

- [x] File begins with the **exact** copyright block (no leading blank line).
- [x] Year is exactly `2026`.

## 2. Import Statement

- [x] Exactly one import line, immediately after the copyright block (separated by one blank line): `import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports`.
- [x] No other imports are present.

## 3. Module Docstring

- [x] A `/-! ... -/` module docstring appears after the import, separated by one blank line.
- [x] Starts with a heading: `# ErdŇĎs Problem 1167`.
- [x] Contains a `*Reference:*` line with a markdown link to `erdosproblems.com/1167`.
- [x] Includes a brief plain-English description of the problem ("A problem of ErdŇĎs, Hajnal, and Rado on cardinal partition relations.").
- [x] Optional bibliography entries: N/A (none needed).

## 4. Namespace

- [x] All declarations are inside `namespace Erdos1167 ... end Erdos1167`.
- [x] Namespace name uses `UpperCamelCase`: `Erdos1167`.
- [x] `open Cardinal` appears **before** the namespace block.

## 5. Custom Definitions

- [x] `CardinalPartitionRel` has a LaTeX docstring (`/-- ... -/`).
- [x] No `noncomputable` needed ‚ÄĒ the definition is a `Prop` and does not involve classical choice or nonconstructive operations.
- [x] No `sorry` in definitions.
- [x] `CardinalPartitionRel` uses `UpperCamelCase` (it is a `Prop`).
- [x] No `local notation` used (none needed for this file).
- [ ] No `@[category test]` or `@[category API]` sanity checks for `CardinalPartitionRel`. *Optional ‚ÄĒ "consider adding" per the checklist; not a blocking issue.*

## 6. Theorem Statements

- [x] Uses the `theorem` keyword.
- [x] Main theorem is named `erdos_1167` (snake_case, matching namespace number).
- [x] Name follows Lean/Mathlib `snake_case` conventions.
- [x] Has both `@[category ...]` and `@[AMS ...]` on the **same attribute line**: `@[category research open, AMS 3 5]`.
- [x] Main theorem has a docstring (`/-- **ErdŇĎs Problem 1167** ... -/`).
- [x] Docstring references the source: "ErdŇĎs‚ÄďHajnal‚ÄďRado".

### `answer()` usage

The original problem asks a yes/no question: *"does 2^őĽ ‚Üí (őļ_őĪ + 1)^{r+1} imply őĽ ‚Üí (őļ_őĪ)^r?"* ‚ÄĒ this is **Case 1**.

- [x] Theorem type is `answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ P`, with `P` formalizing the question as asked.
- [x] Problem is open, so `answer(sorry)` is correct (not `True`/`False`).
- [x] The RHS formalizes the question so that `answer(True)` would mean "yes" and `answer(False)` would mean "no".
- [x] Quantifiers (`‚ąÄ {őĻ : Type*} (őļ : őĻ ‚Üí Cardinal) ...`) appear **after** `answer(sorry)`, not as theorem parameters.

## 7. Category Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[category ...]` attribute present.
- [x] Value is `research open` ‚ÄĒ a valid category.
- [x] `research open` theorem contains `sorry` in its proof body.

## 8. AMS Attribute

- [x] Exactly one `@[AMS ...]` attribute present.
- [x] At least one AMS subject number listed: `3 5`.
- [x] Numbers are in ascending numeric order: 3, 5.
- [x] No duplicate numbers.
- [x] Both codes are valid AMS MSC2020 codes (3 = Mathematical logic and foundations, 5 = Combinatorics).

## 9. Proof Bodies

- [x] Open conjecture uses `by` with `sorry` (multi-line `by\n  sorry` form, which is acceptable).
- [x] No `sorry` in `def`, `abbrev`, or `structure` declarations.
- [x] No informal proof sketches for this open research problem.
- [x] No `native_decide`.

## 10. Variants & Multi-part Problems

- N/A ‚ÄĒ no variants present. Single-part problem.

## 11. Code Quality

- [x] 2-space indentation throughout.
- [x] Unicode math symbols used where applicable (`‚ąÄ`, `‚ąÉ`, `‚Č•`, `‚Č§`, `‚Üí`, `‚ÜĒ`, `‚Ąē`).
- [x] No debug code (`#check`, `#eval`, `#print`, `dbg_trace`).
- [x] No `set_option` disabling linters.
- [x] Lines are reasonably short (all <100 characters).
- [x] File ends with a single trailing newline.

## 12. Build Verification

- [ ] `lake build` not run in this review. Must be verified before merge.
- [ ] All five custom linters not explicitly tested. Must be verified before merge.

---

## Summary

**Result: PASS** (pending build verification)

The file is clean and fully conformant with the style guidelines. The copyright header, import, module docstring, namespace structure, definition docstring, theorem naming, `answer()` usage (Case 1 ‚ÄĒ yes/no question), category/AMS attributes, and proof body all conform to the checklist. The only unchecked items are the optional `@[category test]` sanity check for the custom definition (non-blocking) and build verification (must be confirmed separately).

1‚Üí/-
     2‚ÜíCopyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.
     3‚Üí
     4‚ÜíLicensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
     5‚Üíyou may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
     6‚ÜíYou may obtain a copy of the License at
     7‚Üí
     8‚Üí    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0
     9‚Üí
    10‚ÜíUnless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
    11‚Üídistributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
    12‚ÜíWITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
    13‚ÜíSee the License for the specific language governing permissions and
    14‚Üílimitations under the License.
    15‚Üí-/
    16‚Üí
    17‚Üíimport FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports
    18‚Üí
    19‚Üí/-!
    20‚Üí# ErdŇĎs Problem 1167
    21‚Üí
    22‚Üí*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1167](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1167)
    23‚Üí
    24‚ÜíA problem of ErdŇĎs, Hajnal, and Rado on cardinal partition relations.
    25‚Üí-/
    26‚Üí
    27‚Üíopen Cardinal
    28‚Üí
    29‚Üínamespace Erdos1167
    30‚Üí
    31‚Üí/-- The cardinal partition relation $\kappa \to (\text{targets}(\alpha))_{\alpha : \iota}^r$:
    32‚Üí    for every coloring of the $r$-element subsets of a $\kappa$-sized set with colors from $\iota$,
    33‚Üí    there exists a color $i$ and a monochromatic subset of cardinality $\geq \text{targets}(i)$. -/
    34‚Üídef CardinalPartitionRel (őļ : Cardinal) {őĻ : Type*} (targets : őĻ ‚Üí Cardinal)
    35‚Üí    (r : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
    36‚Üí  ‚ąÄ (S : Type*) [DecidableEq S] (_ : #S = őļ) (c : Finset S ‚Üí őĻ),
    37‚Üí    ‚ąÉ (i : őĻ) (H : Set S),
    38‚Üí      Cardinal.mk H ‚Č• targets i ‚ąß
    39‚Üí      ‚ąÄ s : Finset S, s.card = r ‚Üí (‚ÜĎs : Set S) ‚äÜ H ‚Üí c s = i
    40‚Üí
    41‚Üí/-- **ErdŇĎs Problem 1167** (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďHajnal‚ÄďRado):
    42‚Üí    For $r \geq 2$ and $\lambda$ infinite, does
    43‚Üí    $2^\lambda \to (\kappa_\alpha + 1)^{r+1}$ imply $\lambda \to (\kappa_\alpha)^r$? -/
    44‚Üí@[category research open, AMS 3 5]
    45‚Üítheorem erdos_1167 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    46‚Üí    ‚ąÄ {őĻ : Type*} (őļ : őĻ ‚Üí Cardinal) (lam : Cardinal) (r : ‚Ąē),
    47‚Üí      r ‚Č• 2 ‚Üí ‚ĄĶ‚āÄ ‚Č§ lam ‚Üí
    48‚Üí      CardinalPartitionRel ((2 : Cardinal) ^ lam) (fun őĪ => őļ őĪ + 1) (r + 1) ‚Üí
    49‚Üí      CardinalPartitionRel lam őļ r := by
    50‚Üí  sorry
    51‚Üí
    52‚Üíend Erdos1167
    53‚Üí

file.content:
/-
Copyright 2026 The Formal Conjectures Authors.

Licensed under the Apache License, Version 2.0 (the "License");
you may not use this file except in compliance with the License.
You may obtain a copy of the License at

    https://www.apache.org/licenses/LICENSE-2.0

Unless required by applicable law or agreed to in writing, software
distributed under the License is distributed on an "AS IS" BASIS,
WITHOUT WARRANTIES OR CONDITIONS OF ANY KIND, either express or implied.
See the License for the specific language governing permissions and
limitations under the License.
-/

import FormalConjectures.Util.ProblemImports

/-!
# ErdŇĎs Problem 1167

*Reference:* [erdosproblems.com/1167](https://www.erdosproblems.com/1167)

A problem of ErdŇĎs, Hajnal, and Rado on cardinal partition relations.
-/

open Cardinal

namespace Erdos1167

/-- The cardinal partition relation $\kappa \to (\text{targets}(\alpha))_{\alpha : \iota}^r$:
    for every coloring of the $r$-element subsets of a $\kappa$-sized set with colors from $\iota$,
    there exists a color $i$ and a monochromatic subset of cardinality $\geq \text{targets}(i)$. -/
def CardinalPartitionRel (őļ : Cardinal) {őĻ : Type*} (targets : őĻ ‚Üí Cardinal)
    (r : ‚Ąē) : Prop :=
  ‚ąÄ (S : Type*) [DecidableEq S] (_ : #S = őļ) (c : Finset S ‚Üí őĻ),
    ‚ąÉ (i : őĻ) (H : Set S),
      Cardinal.mk H ‚Č• targets i ‚ąß
      ‚ąÄ s : Finset S, s.card = r ‚Üí (‚ÜĎs : Set S) ‚äÜ H ‚Üí c s = i

/-- **ErdŇĎs Problem 1167** (ErdŇĎs‚ÄďHajnal‚ÄďRado):
    For $r \geq 2$ and $\lambda$ infinite, does
    $2^\lambda \to (\kappa_\alpha + 1)^{r+1}$ imply $\lambda \to (\kappa_\alpha)^r$? -/
@[category research open, AMS 3 5]
theorem erdos_1167 : answer(sorry) ‚ÜĒ
    ‚ąÄ {őĻ : Type*} (őļ : őĻ ‚Üí Cardinal) (lam : Cardinal) (r : ‚Ąē),
      r ‚Č• 2 ‚Üí ‚ĄĶ‚āÄ ‚Č§ lam ‚Üí
      CardinalPartitionRel ((2 : Cardinal) ^ lam) (fun őĪ => őļ őĪ + 1) (r + 1) ‚Üí
      CardinalPartitionRel lam őļ r := by
  sorry

end Erdos1167

content.text: The review summary says **PASS** (pending build verification). No changes needed to `deepmind/1167.lean`.
